rulings.Case Title: EDWARD KOUNTZE ET AL VS. KALLE KARL-HEINZ PIEPER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Defendant Paolo Broggis Special Motion To Strike Plaintiffs' Second Amended Complaint Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure 425.16
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 9, 2022 line 1. NOTICE OF DEFENDANT PAOLO BROGGI'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFFS' SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 425.16 is DENIED. Motion is untimely per CCP 425.16; Hewlett-Packard Co. v. Oracle Corp. (2015) 239 Cal.App.4th 1174. Compare Notice of Motion and June 2, 2020 complaint. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: KEYHAN MOHANNA VS. OCWEN LOAN SERVICING, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 9, 2022 line 2. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. The Court has previously granted a leave to amend to "allege facts in support of each element of each cause of action asserted not based on a prior recorded reconveyance/satisfaction of the Deed of Trust." The amended complaint, as confirmed by arguments in the opposition in the reply is based on the reconveyed/voided/eradicated Deed of Trust theory. (Opposition 5:13-16). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
